mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, boosting revenue growth across consumer discretionary and materials sectors and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a hit rate above 55%, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust labor market expansion, supporting wage growth and consumer confidence, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative 3-month inflation increase above 0.8% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Elevated inflation thresholds fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered cases despite tightening expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a decoupling of output and labor demand, signaling economic fragility that reduces earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence metric shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases during a supply-constrained environment amplify input cost pressures and compress profit margins, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during supply bottlenecks fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal hit rate drops below 45% in this specific regime.
